Assign defining sequences to each component from its exemplar

Source: R/validate-mapping.R
dot-defining_seqs.Rd

Each component's defining sequences are those where it holds the largest share of that sequence's weight across components (argmax over the beta columns), restricted to sequences the component emphasises (its own top sequences). Sequences that are not clearly owned by one component are left unassigned, so a shared backbone sequence does not corrupt a pair's ratio.

Usage

.defining_seqs(beta, top_n = 15, owner_frac = 0.5)

Arguments

beta

components x sequences exemplar matrix.

top_n

Consider each component's top-N sequences as candidates (default 15).

owner_frac

A sequence is "owned" by a component only if that component holds at least this fraction of the sequence's total weight across components (default 0.5 = clear majority).

Value

A named character vector: sequence -> owning component (or dropped).

On this page
